speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Whither away so fast?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: O, God save you.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: E’en to the Hall to hear what shall become

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Of the great Duke of Buckingham.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: I’ll save you

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: That labor, sir. All’s now done but the ceremony

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Of bringing back the prisoner.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Were you there?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Yes, indeed was I.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Pray speak what has happened.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: You may guess quickly what.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Is he found guilty?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Yes, truly, is he, and condemned upon ’t.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: I am sorry for ’t.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: So are a number more.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: But pray, how passed it?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: I’ll tell you in a little. The great duke

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Came to the bar, where to his accusations

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: He pleaded still not guilty and alleged

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Many sharp reasons to defeat the law.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: The King’s attorney on the contrary

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Urged on the examinations, proofs, confessions

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Of divers witnesses, which the Duke desired

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: To him brought viva voce to his face;

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: At which appeared against him his surveyor,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Sir Gilbert Peck his chancellor, and John Car,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Confessor to him, with that devil monk,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Hopkins, that made this mischief.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: That was he

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: That fed him with his prophecies?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: The same.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: All these accused him strongly, which he fain

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Would have flung from him, but indeed he could not.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: And so his peers upon this evidence

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Have found him guilty of high treason. Much

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: He spoke, and learnèdly, for life, but all

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Was either pitied in him or forgotten.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: After all this, how did he bear himself?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: When he was brought again to th’ bar to hear

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: His knell rung out, his judgment, he was stirred

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: With such an agony he sweat extremely

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: And something spoke in choler, ill and hasty.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: But he fell to himself again, and sweetly

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: In all the rest showed a most noble patience.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: I do not think he fears death.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Sure he does not;

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: He never was so womanish. The cause

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: He may a little grieve at.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Certainly

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: The Cardinal is the end of this.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: ’Tis likely,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: By all conjectures; first, Kildare’s attainder,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Then Deputy of Ireland, who, removed,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Earl Surrey was sent thither, and in haste too,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Lest he should help his father.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: That trick of state

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Was a deep envious one.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: At his return

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: No doubt he will requite it. This is noted,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: And generally: whoever the King favors,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: The Card’nal instantly will find employment,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: And far enough from court too.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: All the commons

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Hate him perniciously and, o’ my conscience,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Wish him ten fathom deep. This duke as much

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: They love and dote on, call him bounteous Buckingham,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: The mirror of all courtesy.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Stay there, sir,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: And see the noble ruined man you speak of.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Let’s stand close and behold him.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: All good people,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: You that thus far have come to pity me,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Hear what I say, and then go home and lose me.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: I have this day received a traitor’s judgment,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And by that name must die. Yet heaven bear witness,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And if I have a conscience, let it sink me

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Even as the ax falls, if I be not faithful!

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: The law I bear no malice for my death;

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: ’T has done, upon the premises, but justice.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: But those that sought it I could wish more Christian.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Be what they will, I heartily forgive ’em.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Yet let ’em look they glory not in mischief,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Nor build their evils on the graves of great men,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: For then my guiltless blood must cry against ’em.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: For further life in this world I ne’er hope,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Nor will I sue, although the King have mercies

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: More than I dare make faults. You few that loved me

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And dare be bold to weep for Buckingham,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: His noble friends and fellows, whom to leave

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Is only bitter to him, only dying,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Go with me like good angels to my end,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And as the long divorce of steel falls on me,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Make of your prayers one sweet sacrifice,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And lift my soul to heaven.—Lead on, a’ God’s name.

speaker: LOVELL
text: I do beseech your Grace, for charity,

speaker: LOVELL
text: If ever any malice in your heart

speaker: LOVELL
text: Were hid against me, now to forgive me frankly.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Sir Thomas Lovell, I as free forgive you

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: As I would be forgiven. I forgive all.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: There cannot be those numberless offenses

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: ’Gainst me that I cannot take peace with. No black envy

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Shall make my grave. Commend me to his Grace.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And if he speak of Buckingham, pray tell him

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: You met him half in heaven. My vows and prayers

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Yet are the King’s and, till my soul forsake,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Shall cry for blessings on him. May he live

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Longer than I have time to tell his years.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Ever beloved and loving may his rule be;

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And when old Time shall lead him to his end,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Goodness and he fill up one monument!

speaker: LOVELL
text: To th’ waterside I must conduct your Grace,

speaker: LOVELL
text: Then give my charge up to Sir Nicholas Vaux,

speaker: LOVELL
text: Who undertakes you to your end.

speaker: VAUX
text: Prepare there!

speaker: VAUX
text: The Duke is coming. See the barge be ready,

speaker: VAUX
text: And fit it with such furniture as suits

speaker: VAUX
text: The greatness of his person.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Nay, Sir Nicholas,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Let it alone. My state now will but mock me.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: When I came hither, I was Lord High Constable

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And Duke of Buckingham; now, poor Edward Bohun.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Yet I am richer than my base accusers,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: That never knew what truth meant. I now seal it,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And with that blood will make ’em one day groan for ’t.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: My noble father, Henry of Buckingham,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Who first raised head against usurping Richard,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Flying for succor to his servant Banister,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Being distressed, was by that wretch betrayed,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And, without trial, fell. God’s peace be with him.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Henry the Seventh, succeeding, truly pitying

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: My father’s loss, like a most royal prince

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Restored me to my honors and out of ruins

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Made my name once more noble. Now his son,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Henry the Eighth, life, honor, name, and all

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: That made me happy at one stroke has taken

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Forever from the world. I had my trial,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And must needs say a noble one, which makes me

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: A little happier than my wretched father.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Yet thus far we are one in fortunes: both

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Fell by our servants, by those men we loved most—

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: A most unnatural and faithless service.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Heaven has an end in all; yet, you that hear me,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: This from a dying man receive as certain:

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Where you are liberal of your loves and counsels

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Be sure you be not loose; for those you make friends

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And give your hearts to, when they once perceive

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: The least rub in your fortunes, fall away

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Like water from you, never found again

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: But where they mean to sink you. All good people,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Pray for me. I must now forsake you. The last hour

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Of my long weary life is come upon me.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Farewell. And when you would say something that is sad,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Speak how I fell. I have done; and God forgive me.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: O, this is full of pity, sir! It calls,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: I fear, too many curses on their heads

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: That were the authors.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: If the Duke be guiltless,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: ’Tis full of woe. Yet I can give you inkling

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Of an ensuing evil, if it fall,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Greater than this.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Good angels keep it from us!

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: What may it be? You do not doubt my faith, sir?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: This secret is so weighty ’twill require

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: A strong faith to conceal it.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Let me have it.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: I do not talk much.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: I am confident;

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: You shall, sir. Did you not of late days hear

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: A buzzing of a separation

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Between the King and Katherine?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Yes, but it held not;

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: For when the King once heard it, out of anger

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: He sent command to the Lord Mayor straight

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: To stop the rumor and allay those tongues

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: That durst disperse it.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: But that slander, sir,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Is found a truth now, for it grows again

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Fresher than e’er it was, and held for certain

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: The King will venture at it. Either the Cardinal,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Or some about him near, have, out of malice

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: To the good queen, possessed him with a scruple

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: That will undo her. To confirm this too,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Cardinal Campeius is arrived, and lately,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: As all think, for this business.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: ’Tis the Cardinal;

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: And merely to revenge him on the Emperor

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: For not bestowing on him at his asking

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: The archbishopric of Toledo this is purposed.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: I think you have hit the mark. But is ’t not cruel

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: That she should feel the smart of this? The Cardinal

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Will have his will, and she must fall.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: ’Tis woeful.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: We are too open here to argue this.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Let’s think in private more.
